question: Is the NuScale inservice inspection program adequate for monitoring RPV integrity?

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with specific classifications for different piping systems, and references the use of the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC as the code of record.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, inspection, and quality assurance.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: The section outlines the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, in compliance with technical specifications and based on as-built material properties. It details the evaluation of pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy, confirming that predicted values remain within regulatory limits for the NuScale reactor’s 60-year design life.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, specifying the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components are conducted according to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI requirements, as mandated by federal regulations, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, such as 10 CFR 50 and its appendices. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with detailed specifications provided in Table 5.2-4.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies compliance with ASME BPVC and NQA-1 standards for handling and storage, as well as environmental controls during shipment, and lists the key reference documents used.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are tested during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the procedures for valve exercising and position verification, and specifies that a one-time in-situ DHRS heat removal test will be conducted on the first reactor module during initial startup.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until its source is determined, and leakage rates are monitored through RCS inventory balance and comparison with the containment evacuation system (CES) flow, with operational limits specified in plant technical specifications. The leakage detection systems are designed to be reliable, redundant, and sensitive enough to support safety analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV is a key pressure-retaining component that forms part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, supporting various internal systems and ensuring the integrity and safety of the plant by using materials and fabrication methods compliant with ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing validates the new support design, and typical SG program guidelines remain appropriate, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, serving as a critical barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

edges.description: Section A cites regulatory codes and standards that the inservice inspection program complies with, while Section B lists the specific references (such as ASME and EPRI documents) that provide the technical basis for those codes and standards, indicating that A references B to substantiate its compliance claims.

Section A establishes that the RPV and its components, including threaded fasteners, are designed and inspected per ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B specifies that the threaded fasteners used in the RPV conform to these same ASME BPVC requirements, thus providing detailed evidence supporting the general compliance claim in Section A.

Section A lists references, including industry standards and guidelines, that are foundational to the technical and procedural requirements described in Section B for ensuring reactor vessel integrity and compliance with operational limits.

Section A lists references, including standards and guidelines, that are cited to support the technical and regulatory basis for the inservice inspection and material selection practices described in Section B.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, while Section B details the material selection and inspection requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, both contributing to the overall adequacy of the inservice inspection program for maintaining RPV integrity.

Section A states that RCPB materials conform to ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section B specifies that the RPV and associated components are designed, fabricated, tested, and inspected according to ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a, thus Section B provides the general regulatory framework that Section A's material conformance details support.

Section A describes how inservice inspection requirements of Section XI of the BPVC are applied to certain components, while Section B states that the RPV and pressure-retaining components comply with 10 CFR 50.55a and are inspected as Class 1 per Section III of the BPVC, thus Section A provides specific inspection practices that support the general regulatory compliance claim in Section B.

Section A describes the inservice inspection requirements applied to certain reactor coolant system components, while Section B lists references including industry guidelines and standards that underpin or inform those inspection requirements, connecting them through regulatory and technical basis.
